mentions.text:
My top 15 catalyst driven stocks from September sorted by potential to FY 2025, explanation, and ideal entry points:

1. $NBIS - 17B+ MSFT contract, dilution mostly finished < $100 (now). I like this over $IREN, $WULF, or $BTBT
2. $HIMS - 42% Short interest. 1.2M+ customers coming in from EU.  Could up to $100 on surprise news <  $45
3. $LTC - ETF launch soon + LTC reserves < $120 (now) 
4. $RKLB - Neutron Launch early 2026 < $42
5. $TSM (calls)- Keep growing to 1.8T+ MC, capex spend insane.< $265 (now)
6. $ETOR - Undervalued with fintechl ike $HOOD $IBKR taking off. I'd prefer this over $DLO or $DAVE at current prices  < $48 (now)
7. $LULU - Short term bounce to year end from holiday consumer spending, oversold < $165 (now)
7. $MRVL - Healthy 45-55% Y/Y, unfairly punished after earnings < $70 (now)
8. $SG - Worth the investment for bounceback. $45 last year. < $9 (now)
9. $TSSI - Server racks like $SMCI, $DELL catching up to rest of AI boom. < $13.5 
10. $ASTS - Cult like stock like $OPEN < $38.5
11. $CRDO - Catchup to $ALAB at $38B MC < $125
12. $RUM - Charlie Kirk + conservatives likely to drive engagement to platform EOY. < $7.25
13. $AMZN (calls)- AWS... Follow $ORCL and other datacenter earnings < $215
14. $SMCI - 33B forward revenue 2026. 24B market cap lol. One good earnings and it pops off. $45 (now)
15. $IBIT (1Y leaps) - Pure BTC given USD printing. No $MSTR or $BMNR, just pure asset ETF < $112.5k

Anything else I'm missing?

reasons: people should ignore it and hold the underlying asset instead
mentions.text:
@matthew_sigel Yeah $WYFI is a great buy. People should just ignore $BTBT and hold the underlying asset.

Companies can be below NAV for many reasons, just look at Grayscale and its crypto holdings for many years

reasons: former miner that spun off WYFI as HPC pivot
mentions.text:
@dotsamaenjoyer If you didn’t know, $WYFi is a HPC spinoff of the former miner $BTBT and they repurposed bitcoin data centers into AI compute.

This is what the post was about how not every former miner pivoting into HPC will be successful when it comes to scale, margins,  and execution.

reasons: miner and majority owner of WYFI
miner moving into HPC via WYFI spinoff
miner and majority owner of WYFI
repurposed some assets from mining into HPC
down -11.51% as part of miner sell-off
narrative changes around margins vs. capacity contributing to miner sell-off
mentions.text:
@midlevelcruiser @SathyeshBhat This isn't just about $IREN, it's around HPC miners. If anything IREN is probably one of the more exceptional performers, especially vs. $CLSK, $BITF, and others.

EG.
$NBIS: +7.25%
_
$CIFR: -7.15%
$RIOT: -11.5%
$BITF: -19.92%
$CLSK: -11.63%
$IREN: -5.54%
$BTBT: -11.51%

Again, it's probably multi-faceted around Bitcoin prices. But it's pretty apparent as of recently, there's been divergence when normally they would move together (with $NBIS, $CRWV) lower beta. 

And I'd cite narrative changes around margins vs. capacity being a part of miner sell-off and consolidation into certain Neoclouds.

reasons: down -11.51% as part of miner divergence
mentions.text:
@gary_pallett You're misunderstanding the nuance of the post when you say 6 months, 12 months, 3 years.      

The point of the post was that we're now seeing early divergence of the names that usually trade in sync + $NBIS and others that trade lower beta.      

This week: $NBIS: +7.25% | $CIFR: -7.15% $RIOT: -11.5% $BITF: -19.92% $CLSK: -11.63% $IREN: -5.54% $BTBT: -11.51%.    

This is just an observation from about the divergence. And I wanted to point out that it's likely margin differences that the market's starting to price in.    

When the Neocloud leaders make posts about full-stack margins like these, markets react.
